titel: The Swedish National Audit Office's report on infiltration within the government.
html:
In this document, the government outlines its assessment of the conclusions and recommendations presented in the report "Infiltration in the State – The work of three agencies to counter disloyal behavior" (RiR 2026:3) by the Swedish National Audit Office.

The Swedish National Audit Office has reviewed whether the Swedish Defence Materiel Administration, the National Agency for Public Procurement, and the Swedish Prison and Probation Service are taking effective measures to counter infiltration. The National Audit Office's overall assessment is that the work of the agencies being reviewed to counter infiltration is, as a whole, not effective.

The Swedish National Audit Office does not make any recommendations to the government. However, recommendations are made to the agencies to improve their work against infiltration.

The government welcomes the Swedish National Audit Office's review. Security work needs to be prioritized to prevent organized crime from occurring in our public institutions. The government has taken several measures that, collectively, address the areas for improvement identified in the Swedish National Audit Office's review. The government is also following up on the various development efforts being carried out in the area of infiltration through dialogue with the agencies.

With this document, the government considers the Swedish National Audit Office's report to be finalized.
tldr: The Swedish National Audit Office has investigated how three government agencies are working to prevent infiltration, which refers to attempts by external parties to improperly influence the agencies. The findings indicate that the agencies' efforts are not yet sufficient.
lattlast:
The Swedish National Audit Office is an agency that monitors whether the state is doing its job correctly. They have reviewed how the Swedish Defence Materiel Administration, the National Archives of Sweden, and the Swedish Prison and Probation Service operate to prevent infiltration. Infiltration refers to individuals or groups attempting to gain control over or influence government agencies' decisions in an insecure manner.

The review shows that the agencies' overall work is not effective. This means they are not doing enough to prevent disloyal behavior. The National Audit Office therefore provides recommendations to the agencies on how they can improve their security work to protect the state.

The government has responded to the report and stated that they believe the review is good. They emphasize that it is important to prioritize security work so that organized crime cannot influence our public authorities. The government also states that they are already taking steps to improve the areas identified by the National Audit Office.
